description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and a wide base for stability. It features three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one on the front, each capped with a hexagonal nut. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut or cap, used for opening the valve to release water. The surface is uniformly painted in a bright, glossy red, typical for high-visibility emergency equipment in urban environments. The hydrant is constructed from heavy-duty cast metal, likely iron or steel, designed to withstand high water pressure and external impacts. No chains, manufacturer markings, or additional accessories are visible on the exterior.
description: A bright red, cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, made of heavy cast metal. Its surface is glossy and smooth, with no visible markings or chains.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed top, central operating nut, and the prominent front hose outlet with two side outlets visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the side hose outlets and the domed top, with the front and rear outlets partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets visible and the front outlet hidden.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side hose outlet and the domed top, with the front and rear outlets partially visible.
